prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) computed at the GFN2-xTB level (xtb backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet). N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges: Mulliken (GFN2-xTB). All three states converged; integrity trustworthy = true; Σ(f⁺) = Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000. No warnings reported by the engine.

Headline result: Fe (atom 11) is both the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191), with a negative dual descriptor (f⁺−f⁻ = −0.030527354), confirming dominant nucleophilic character at the iron center — consistent with ferrocene's well-known electron-donor behaviour. Both Cp rings give identical Fukui values (f⁺ ≈ 0.01364 per C, ≈ 0.06348 per H), reflecting the molecular symmetry.

Method: GFN2-xTB (Bannwarth, C.; Ehlert, S.; Grimme, S. J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019, 15, 1652–1671). CLI: cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 ferrocene.xyz. Plot: .../agent_call_01_plot.png.
